The previous post covered `@import`. This one starts pulling in earlier topics, building up to `mixin`. What's a mixin? I think of it as an SCSS library — a place to store frequently used SCSS snippets.
JavaScript Hoisting: Why Does That Variable Say undefined?

During a JavaScript Q&A session I realized my mental model of hoisting was a little off, so I wrote this down to set it straight. This post walks through what hoisting actually is, why a variable can log undefined before it's assigned, how function declarations get hoisted, and how it all ties back to memory.
SCSS provides `@import`, which makes development management way easier when used well.
this Is the Weirdest Thing in JavaScript — Here's How I Finally Got It

When I was learning JavaScript, one of the most painful, head-scratching things was `this`. It changes depending on where and how you call it, and that drove me up the wall. So I sat down and worked through a bunch of examples to figure out how `this` actually behaves — global scope, inside objects, strict mode, and the arrow function gotcha that bit me in real code.
Operators? Like add, subtract, multiply, divide? Yep — SCSS supports operators: add (+), subtract (-), multiply (*), and divide (/).
How I Merged Array Object Properties in JavaScript (and Burned 4 Hours Doing It)

A friend threw a JavaScript problem at me to chew on — he handed me an array of objects and asked me to merge the ones sharing the same id. Looked simple. Ended up taking me about 3 to 4 hours. This post walks through every wrong turn I took and how I finally landed on a clean solution.
From this post on we won't talk about SASS — let's focus on the SCSS basics.

Why am I writing this? Since switching to frontend engineering and joining HexSchool, friends, online friends, and classmates have kept asking me how to learn frontend like I did. I thought about it for a while before writing this experience-sharing post.
